commit.message:
fix(agent): migrate Foundry agent to V2 and fix version/tool serialization (#9036)

* feat(agent): migrate Foundry agent from V1 to V2 (FoundryAgentServiceV2)

Update agentModelType dropdown value from FoundryAgentService to
FoundryAgentServiceV2 to enable the Responses API code path. Add
foundryVersionName field, remove V1-only fields (foundryAgentId,
foundryAgentVersion, foundryAgentVersionNumber), and update all
visibility conditions, serialization helpers, and tests across
designer, designer-v2, and logic-apps-shared packages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(agent): add Foundry V2 regression tests

Add regression tests to prevent V1 references from being reintroduced:
- agentloop.spec.ts: validates manifest dropdown, V2 fields, V1 field
  removal, deploymentId exclusion, no leftover V1 visibility refs
- foundryV2Contract.spec.ts (designer-v2): validates ManifestToDisplayName
  mapping, isFoundryAgentNameParameter, no V1 string literals
- foundryV2Contract.spec.ts (designer): same contract tests for v1 lib

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(agent): address PR review feedback

- Fix foundryVersionName default from '' to 'v1' in designer v1
- Rename rawFoundryAgentId/prevRawAgentIdRef to rawFoundryAgentName/
  prevRawAgentNameRef in both designers for clarity
- Accept both FoundryAgentService (V1) and FoundryAgentServiceV2 in
  isAgentConnectorAndAgentServiceModel for backward compat with
  existing workflows
- Add backward compat test cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(agent): fix V2 loading, agent dropdown, knowledge base, and system message

- Fix infinite loading spinner: replace isLoading with isFetching for
  TanStack Query v4 compatibility (disabled queries report isLoading=true)
- Remove RBAC gate for Foundry proxy queries — rely on query retry logic
  for auth errors instead
- Show agent picker dropdown: set foundryAgentName to x-ms-visibility
  important with combobox editor
- Hide knowledge base for V2: add visibility condition excluding
  FoundryAgentServiceV2
- Strip system messages from V2 workflow JSON at serializer level —
  instructions live on the Foundry agent definition, not in workflow
- Revert backward compat in isAgentConnectorAndAgentServiceModel (V2 only)
- Rename stale variable names (rawFoundryAgentId -> rawFoundryAgentName)
- Fix foundryVersionName default from empty string to v1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(agent): improve Foundry loading states and agent picker reactivity

- Show loading spinner on agent dropdown while foundryAgentsForNode is
  undefined (null-check instead of isFetching/isLoading flags)
  consistently across both loading checkpoints
- Add React key to SettingsSection that includes agent count to force
  re-render when agents load

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(agent): change foundryVersionName default to v2 and stop writing agent version numbers

foundryVersionName represents the Foundry agent service version (v2 for
Responses API), not the individual agent's version number. The version
dropdown is UI-only for loading the correct model/instructions.

- Change manifest default from 'v1' to 'v2'
- Remove all dispatchParamUpdate calls that wrote agent version numbers
  into foundryVersionName (init effect, refresh effect, agent switch,
  version change handler)
- Stop reading foundryVersionName from Redux in effectiveFoundryVersion
- Update dependent defaults from 'v1' to 'v2' in both designers
- Update agentloop.spec.ts assertion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(agent): serialize foundryVersionName as v{N} from agent version dropdown

- Write version.version with 'v' prefix (e.g. 'v7') to foundryVersionName
  instead of version.name (which returns the agent name)
- Restore reading/writing foundryVersionName to workflow params for the
  backend to reference the correct agent version
- Match stored foundryVersionName against v{version} format when
  restoring effective version from Redux
- Use empty default instead of 'v1' in addFoundryDependentUpdates to
  prevent false match against version 1 — ensures latest version is
  selected on first agent add
- Clean up stale .name guard in init effect condition

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(agent): address PR feedback and add regression tests

- Rename isAgentConnectorAndFoundryAgentId to
  isAgentConnectorAndFoundryAgentName across both designers and tests
- Change foundryVersionName dependent default from '' to undefined so
  buildDependentParam creates the dependency correctly
- Add serializerFoundryV2.spec.ts: tests V2 system message stripping
  (9 cases covering V2 strip, non-V2 preserve, case insensitivity)
- Add connectionsAgentV2.spec.ts: tests connection model type resolution
  (8 cases covering display name mapping, Foundry fallback, existing
  value preservation)
- Extend agentloop.spec.ts: add knowledgeBaseName V2 exclusion,
  foundryVersionName contract (v{N} format, internal visibility)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(agent): simplify Foundry V2 parameter tab code

- Remove dead foundryRbacReady variable (always true after RBAC removal)
- Remove unused _agentId/_agentName params from addFoundryDependentUpdates
- Eliminate duplicate selectedVersionData lookup in version init effect
- Fix agent matching in v2: use a.name || a.id pattern for robustness

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(agent): stop eagerly writing foundryVersionName default before versions load

Remove addFoundryDependentUpdates from writing foundryVersionName — it
was eagerly setting 'v1' (from schema default) before versions loaded,
causing the dropdown to select version 1 instead of the latest.
foundryVersionName is now exclusively written by the version init effect
after versions are fetched from the API. Remove now-unused
buildDependentParam callback in designer-v2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(agent): remove manifest default for foundryVersionName

The schema default 'v1' was pre-populating the parameter on node
creation, causing effectiveFoundryVersion to match version 1 instead
of falling through to the latest. Remove the default entirely — the
version init effect writes the correct v{N} value after versions load.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
engineering: Allow other ESP paths in validation (#586)

# 🔍 Description

Previously, the ESP (EFI System Partition) mount point was **hardcoded**
to `/boot/efi`. This PR makes the ESP mount path **configurable** via a
new `OverrideEspMount` enum on `FileSystem`, allowing Linux distros that
mount the ESP at `/boot`, `/efi`, or other paths to be serviced by
Trident.

## Behavior Changes

### Per-filesystem `is_esp` flag and `OverrideEspMount`
- New `is_esp: bool` field on `FileSystem` — replaces the removed
`FileSystem::is_esp()` method that compared mount points against a
hardcoded constant
- New `OverrideEspMount` enum (`UseDefault`, `Override`, `Block`)
serialized as `overrideEspMount` on each filesystem entry:
- **`UseDefault`** (default, omitted from JSON): `is_esp` is
auto-detected by comparing the mount point to `/boot/efi`
- **`Override`**: explicitly marks the filesystem as the ESP regardless
of mount path
- **`Block`**: explicitly marks the filesystem as *not* the ESP even if
mounted at `/boot/efi`
- Custom `TryFrom<FileSystemSerde> for FileSystem` / `From<FileSystem>
for FileSystemSerde` drives this logic during (de)serialization

### ESP validation in storage graph
- `check_filesystems()` now discovers the ESP mount path from the graph
and returns it as `Option<PathBuf>`
- New `StorageGraphBuildError::FilesystemEspMultiple` — error when two
filesystems are marked as ESP
- New `StorageGraphBuildError::FilesystemEspWithoutMountPoint` — error
when ESP filesystem has no mount point
- Removed `FilesystemIncompatibleSource` and
`FilesystemVerityIncompatible` error variants

### Host configuration validation
- `validate_inner()` obtains ESP mount path from
`StorageGraph::esp_mount_path()` instead of using a hardcoded constant
- New `HostConfigurationStaticValidationError::EspMountPointNotFound` —
no filesystem is marked as ESP
- `expected_partition_type()` and `check_partition_types()` receive the
ESP mount path as a parameter
- Removed `ImageApiMixed`, `InvalidSourceUrl`,
`UnsupportedSourceUrlScheme`, and `UsrVerityRequiresUkiSupport` error
variants (unrelated cleanup)

### COSI-derived host configuration
- `derive_host_configuration_inner()` now detects ESP partitions by GPT
partition type, sets `is_esp: true` on the first ESP filesystem found,
and warns if multiple ESP partitions with mount points are present

### `PartitionType` improvements
- New `PartitionType::as_dps()` and `From<PartitionType> for
DiscoverablePartitionType` — replaces the duplicated
`config_part_type_into_discoverable()` in `repart_mode.rs`

### Removed storage rule
- The ESP-specific storage rule in `docbuilder` (`filesystems.rs`) has
been removed since the ESP is now validated through the graph builder
(`check_filesystems`)

## Files with meaningful changes

| File | What changed |
|------|-------------|
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/filesystem.rs` | Core of
the PR: `is_esp: bool` field, `OverrideEspMount` enum, `FileSystemSerde`
with custom `TryFrom`/`From` impls for (de)serialization logic |
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/mod.rs` |
`esp_filesystem()` uses `is_esp` flag; `validate_inner()` gets ESP path
from graph; new tests for ESP with adopted partitions, non-default mount
paths (`/boot`, `/efi`), and missing ESP |
|
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/builder/filesystems.rs`
| `check_filesystems()` returns ESP mount path; validates no-mount-point
ESP and multiple-ESP errors |
|
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/builder/mod.rs`
| `StorageGraphBuilder` stores `esp_mount_path`; passes it to
`check_partition_types()` |
|
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/builder/partition.rs`
| `check_partition_types()` and `expected_partition_type()` call sites
receive `esp_mount_path` |
|
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/rules/mod.rs`
| `expected_partition_type()` signature takes `esp_mount_path` instead
of using the hardcoded constant |
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/error.rs` |
New `FilesystemEspMultiple`, `FilesystemEspWithoutMountPoint`; removed
`FilesystemIncompatibleSource`, `FilesystemVerityIncompatible` |
|
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/validation_tests.rs`
| New tests for ESP validation; `is_esp` field added to existing test
fixtures |
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/error.rs` | New
`EspMountPointNotFound`; removed `ImageApiMixed`, `InvalidSourceUrl`,
`UnsupportedSourceUrlScheme`, `UsrVerityRequiresUkiSupport` |
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/partitions.rs` |
`PartitionType::as_dps()` + `From<PartitionType> for
DiscoverablePartitionType` |
| `crates/trident/src/osimage/cosi/derived_hc.rs` | ESP detection during
COSI derivation via GPT partition type; warns on multiple ESP
partitions; new test for multi-ESP behavior |
| `crates/trident/src/engine/storage/partitioning/repart_mode.rs` |
Deleted `config_part_type_into_discoverable()`, replaced with
`partition.partition_type.as_dps()` |
| `crates/docbuilder/src/schema_renderer/node.rs` | `# INTERNAL` tag
stripping logic so internal-only doc comments are excluded from rendered
docs |
| `crates/docbuilder/src/host_config/storage_rules/filesystems.rs` |
Removed ESP-specific storage rule (now handled in graph validation) |
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/filesystem_types.rs` |
Minor cleanup |
| `crates/trident_api/schemas/host-config-schema.json` | New
`overrideEspMount` property on `FileSystem`; `# INTERNAL` tags in schema
descriptions |
| `docs/Reference/Host-Configuration/API-Reference/OverrideEspMount.md`
| New doc page for `OverrideEspMount` enum |
| `docs/Reference/Host-Configuration/API-Reference/Storage.md` | Updated
for new field |
| `docs/Reference/Host-Configuration/Storage-Rules.md` | Removed ESP
storage rule entry |
| `report/esp-constant-usages.md` | Investigation tracking document for
ESP constant usage migration |

## Files with only minor `is_esp` field additions

These files only add `is_esp: true` or `is_esp: false` to `FileSystem`
struct literals in tests, samples, or runtime construction sites:

- `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/mod.rs`
- `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/graph.rs`
-
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/cardinality.rs`
-
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/conversions.rs`
- `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/display.rs`
-
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/documentation.rs`
- `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/types.rs`
- `crates/trident_api/src/samples/sample_hc.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/engine/boot/grub.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/engine/bootentries.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/engine/context/image.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/engine/context/mod.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/engine/newroot.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/engine/rollback.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/engine/storage/filesystem.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/engine/storage/rebuild.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/engine/storage/verity.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/offline_init/mod.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/subsystems/selinux.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/subsystems/storage/fstab.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/subsystems/storage/mod.rs`
- `crates/trident/src/subsystems/storage/osimage.rs`

* Initial plan

* fix: use synthetic string paths in test_dir.py to avoid Windows path issues

Replace tmp_path-based paths with string paths in TestGetProjectNameFromDir
since get_project_name_from_dir() only reads .name and never touches the
filesystem. This avoids Windows-specific failures where directory names like
"..." are interpreted as parent traversal.

commit.message:
perf(maglev): re-enable Maglev execution with dead arguments elimination

Re-enable try_execute_maglev() which was disabled because every
sloppy-mode function's CreateMappedArguments fell to the catch-all
deopt in codegen.  The new replace_dead_arguments() optimizer pass
replaces unused CreateMapped/UnmappedArguments nodes with
UndefinedConstant in-place, allowing functions that never read
'arguments' to stay in JIT.

Changes:
- Add replace_dead_arguments() pass to Maglev optimizer (before DCE)
- Restore full try_execute_maglev() implementation

commit.message:
perf(interp): optimize closure call path for closure_counter_1k benchmark

Three targeted optimizations for the closure call hot path:

1. Raise INLINE_BYTECODE_THRESHOLD from 40 to 120 so more real-world
   closures (counter callbacks, event handlers) take the fast inline
   path and avoid frame allocation + register pool overhead.

2. Add Smi fast path in inline_context_slot_update and
   inline_chained_context_slot_update for AddSmi/SubSmi opcodes.
   Previously, even Smi values went through to_number() -> f64 ->
   number_to_jsvalue() round-trip. Now Smi + Smi stays in integer
   arithmetic with checked_add/checked_sub, only falling back to
   f64 on overflow.

3. Specialize CallUndefinedReceiver0 dispatch: when a closure already
   has JIT code (Maglev/Turbofan), try JIT execution immediately
   before the inline pattern matching and tiering logic. This avoids
   redundant bytecode decoding and invocation count bookkeeping for
   hot closures that have already been JIT-compiled.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(dna): read brand DNA from client_memories (dna_document) as primary source

Root cause of generic copy: getClientDNA() only read client_dna table
which had all fields null. The full Brand DNA (Identidade Visual, Tipografia,
Brand Voice, Palavras Proibidas, etc.) was stored in client_memories
with source='dna_document' — same source Oracle uses via dna-context.ts.

Changes to lib/atlas/dna.ts:
- Add client_memories query (source IN dna_document, dna_field, knowledge_file)
- Priority order for brand_voice_text:
  1. dna_document from client_memories (full Brand DNA curated doc)
  2. brandvoice assets from client_assets (individual pillars)
  3. brand_voice_text from client_dna table
- Add styleguide_text from knowledge_file memories with STYLE GUIDE content
- All 4 queries run in parallel via Promise.all

This means VERA now receives the complete brand identity document

## Why this PR exists
The weekly TASK-116 Copilot repair lane produced a bounded compatibility
fix for a red/manual-review Dependabot update.

- `chore(deps): bump lucide-react from 0.563.0 to 1.7.0`
- head branch: `dependabot/npm_and_yarn/lucide-react-1.7.0`

**lucide-react 0.563.0 -> 1.7.0** - one breaking change found and
resolved.

`lucide-react` removed all brand icons in v1.x, including `Github`. The
only affected file was `components/app-metadata-pill.tsx`, which used
`<Github>` to render a GitHub repository link icon.

**Fix:** Removed the `Github` import and replaced it with a local
`GithubIcon` inline SVG component using the official GitHub mark path.
All other icons used across the codebase (`PlusSquare`, `TriangleAlert`,
`Columns3`, etc.) are present in v1.7.0 and required no changes.

All 530 unit tests pass after the fix.

- `package-lock.json`
- `package.json`

- 3 files changed, 22 insertions(+), 7 deletions(-)

- `npm test -- --run`
- `npm run build`

## CI note
- Required checks are dispatched explicitly after PR creation so this
superseding PR becomes mergeable under the normal branch protection
rules.
- Review and merge remain human-owned.

fix: add Managed Identity Operator role for platform MI kubelet identity

Azure requires the cluster control-plane identity to have Managed Identity
Operator on the kubelet identity before it can assign it to worker nodes.
Since we use the same MI for both roles (mi-{project}-platform), this is a
self-referential assignment.

Without it, AKS creation fails with CustomKubeletIdentityMissingPermissionError.
Added depends_on on azurerm_kubernetes_cluster so the role is in place
before AKS provisioning begins.

commit.message:
fix(types,cli): fail closed on unsupported inferred signatures (#890)

* fix(types,cli): promote Unsupported to fatal and prune error-bearing fn_sigs (#789)

- Promote `TypeExprConversionKind::Unsupported` to fatal in
  `inferred_type_serialization_diagnostic_is_fatal` so unsupported
  inferred types (e.g. generators) abort serialization instead of
  warning and continuing.
- Update `validate_checker_output_contract` to retain only `fn_sigs`
  that pass `signature_uses_unsupported_type` in addition to the
  existing inference-var check, pruning any signature carrying
  `Ty::Error` before it can reach codegen.
- Update doc comment on `TypeExprConversionKind::Unsupported` and the
  adjacent test comment to reflect the new fatal semantics.
- Add regression test in `compile.rs` verifying that a generator type
  (the canonical Unsupported example) produces a fatal diagnostic.
- Add regression test in `admissibility.rs` verifying that fn_sigs with
  `Ty::Error` in params or return type are removed by the contract check.

* refactor(types): rename signature_uses_unsupported_type -> signature_contains_error_type

The helper only checks for Ty::Error via ty_contains_error; the old name
implied broader unsupported-type coverage that does not exist. Rename all
call sites (admissibility.rs, stdlib_loader.rs) and the test function to
match the actual behaviour.

commit.message:
chore(task-116): supersede Dependabot PR #135 (#158)

Supersedes Dependabot PR #135
(https://github.com/dorianagaesse/nexus_dash/pull/135).

## Why this PR exists
The weekly TASK-116 Copilot repair lane produced a bounded compatibility
fix for a red/manual-review Dependabot update.

## Original Dependabot update
- `chore(deps-dev): bump tailwindcss from 3.4.19 to 4.2.2`
- head branch: `dependabot/npm_and_yarn/tailwindcss-4.2.2`

## What the repair lane changed
Tailwind CSS v4 dropped its bundled PostCSS plugin and changed several
config APIs. Four changes were required:

1. **Install `@tailwindcss/postcss`** and update `postcss.config.mjs` to
use `@tailwindcss/postcss` instead of `tailwindcss` as the PostCSS
plugin.
2. **Update `app/globals.css`** - replace deprecated `@tailwind
base/components/utilities` directives with `@import "tailwindcss"` and
add `@config "../tailwind.config.ts"` (v4 compat shim) plus `@plugin
"tailwindcss-animate"`.
3. **Update `tailwind.config.ts`** - remove `tailwindcss-animate` from
the `plugins` array (now loaded via `@plugin` in CSS) and change
`darkMode: ["class"]` to `darkMode: "class"` to match the updated v4
TypeScript type signature.

Build now compiles successfully (Compiled successfully, Finished
TypeScript). The only remaining build failure is Missing required
environment variable: DATABASE_URL, which is a pre-existing
env-validation requirement unrelated to this upgrade.

## Files changed
- `app/globals.css`
- `package-lock.json`
- `package.json`
- `postcss.config.mjs`
- `tailwind.config.ts`

## Diff summary
- 5 files changed, 617 insertions(+), 485 deletions(-)

## Validation reported by the repair lane
- `npm install`
- `npm run build`

## CI note
- Required checks are dispatched explicitly after PR creation so this
superseding PR becomes mergeable under the normal branch protection
rules.
- Review and merge remain human-owned.

commit.message:
docs: rescue carb inference experiments (EXP-1641–1648)

8 experiments characterizing rescue carb detectability:
- EXP-1641: Logistic model best fits recovery (R²=0.78 median)
- EXP-1642: Binary detection feasible at 10 min (AUC=0.81)
- EXP-1643: Magnitude estimation FAILS (r≈0)
- EXP-1644: Counter-regulatory floor = 1.68 mg/dL/step
- EXP-1645: Glycogen adds marginal ΔR²=0.03
- EXP-1646: Forecast improves at 15min (+8%) but hurts at 60min (-39%)
- EXP-1647: Cross-patient transfer FAILS (LOPO R²=-1.02)
- EXP-1648: Practical detector F1=0.91 at 20min, 43% post-hypo hyperglycemia

Key finding: detection-estimation disconnect — can detect rescue carb
occurrence but cannot estimate magnitude from glucose alone.

fix: OPL3 multi-timbral channel routing + scratch mode grace period

Two fixes for AdLib playback:

1. OPL3 channel routing: The OPL3Synth was a mono-timbral keyboard synth
   using round-robin voice allocation. For 9-channel AdLib tracker
   playback, all channels fought over the same voices with the same
   patch. Now the WASM bridge has channel-addressed API
   (oplChannelSetPatch/NoteOn/NoteOff), the worklet routes chSetPatch/
   chNoteOn/chNoteOff messages, OPL3Synth.setChannel() selects the
   target voice, and ToneEngine calls setChannel before each note
   (same pattern as FurnaceSynth/FurnaceDispatchSynth).

2. Scratch mode grace period: A stray trackpad scroll event during file
   load immediately activated scratch mode, muting the replayer and
   stopping playback after ~2 seconds. Added 800ms grace period after
   playback starts where scratch activation is suppressed.

tracked turns after the extension loaded. If the extension restarted

commit.message:
Use Firebase UID as Firestore user key

Migrate Firestore user documents to use Firebase Auth UID as the document ID and add support for storing that UID locally. Key changes:

- Add firebase_uid column to SQLite users table, with migration and helper methods (getFirebaseUid/setFirebaseUid).
- Update DatabaseHelper to persist firebase_uid, use it for all Firestore sync operations (role, profile, password, notif_pref, email_verified), handle profile_image sentinel "DELETED", and ensure deleteUserAccount removes the Firestore doc by UID.
- Change FirestoreHelper to use firebaseUid as document ID, guard against missing UID, switch updateUserField/saveFcmToken/updatePassword/deleteUser to safer set(merge)/guarded flows, and log clearer messages.
- Update login/register flows to store and propagate Firebase UID, keep Firebase Auth signed in where appropriate, perform silent sign-in to ensure Firestore writes succeed, and upload pending FCM tokens by UID.
- Update realtime sync and sync manager to pass the document ID (firebaseUid) into local upsert so local rows store the UID.
- Update FCM sender to query users collection by student_id and handle query results to find the document's fcm_token.
- Start/stop RealtimeSyncManager in UserManagementActivity to refresh UI on remote changes.
- Add KEY_FIREBASE_UID to SessionManager and related save/getters.
- Update .gitignore to exclude service account keys and related files.

Also includes minor generated/build artifact updates (Gradle caches, problems report).

commit.message:
Add repo-commander copilot agent and coding agent environment setup (#299)

Adds a full-access Copilot custom agent (`repo-commander`) that
orchestrates PR management, repo settings, CI/CD, and specialist
dispatch. Also adds `copilot-setup-steps.yml` so the Copilot coding
agent gets a functional environment matching CI.

### New files

- **`.github/agents/repo-commander.agent.md`** — Top-level operations
agent with `read`, `search`, `edit`, `terminal`, `github` tools. Covers
PR triage/review/merge gating, repo settings audit, CI debugging, code
quality enforcement, issue management, and security compliance.
Automatically dispatches to the four existing specialist agents based on
changed-file scope. Includes structured JSON merge-decision contract.

- **`.github/copilot-setup-steps.yml`** — Coding agent environment: Node
22 + pnpm 10.32.1 (corepack), Python 3.12 + uv 0.11.4 (pinned), engine
venv with dev deps. Mirrors `ci.yml` setup steps.

### Updated files

- **`.github/agents/README.md`** — Documents commander → specialist
hierarchy with ASCII architecture diagram and trigger-mapping table.
- **`.github/copilot-instructions.md`** — Registers `repo-commander` in
the custom agents list.

### Agent architecture

```
┌──────────────────────────┐
│     repo-commander       │  ← full access, top-level orchestrator
│  (PRs, settings, CI/CD)  │
└────────┬─────────────────┘
         │ dispatches
    ┌────┴────┬──────────────┬──────────────────┐
    ▼         ▼              ▼                  ▼
platform-  runtime-     supabase-         pr-owner-
sync-      smoke-       boundary-         operator
auditor    guardian      guardian
```

fix: use fresh OIDC token in KV import step, fail fast on import error

The ARM_OIDC_TOKEN pipeline variable holds the token captured at the Azure
Login step. By the time the Import step runs, this token may have expired.
AzureCLI@2 with addSpnToEnvironment injects a fresh $idToken — export it
over ARM_OIDC_TOKEN and ARM_CLIENT_ID so terraform import uses valid auth.

Also fail fast (exit 1) if terraform import fails instead of silently
continuing into terraform plan which then errors with 'already exists'.

commit.message:
chore(task-116): supersede Dependabot PR #133 (#153)

Supersedes Dependabot PR #133
(https://github.com/dorianagaesse/nexus_dash/pull/133).

## Why this PR exists
The weekly TASK-116 Copilot repair lane produced a bounded compatibility
fix for a red/manual-review Dependabot update.

## Original Dependabot update
- `chore(deps-dev): bump prisma from 5.22.0 to 7.6.0`
- head branch: `dependabot/npm_and_yarn/prisma-7.6.0`

## What the repair lane changed
Prisma 7 introduced three breaking changes that required repair:

1. **`@prisma/client` version mismatch** – Dependabot bumped `prisma`
CLI to 7.6.0 but left `@prisma/client` at `^5.0.0`. Both must match.
Fixed by bumping `@prisma/client` to `^7.6.0`.

2. **Mandatory driver adapter** – Prisma 7 removed the legacy binary
engine; `PrismaClient` now requires an `adapter` or `accelerateUrl`.
Added `@prisma/adapter-pg` + `pg` and updated `lib/prisma.ts` to
instantiate via `new PrismaPg(DATABASE_URL)`.

3. **Datasource URL moved out of `schema.prisma`** – Prisma 7 no longer
accepts `url`/`directUrl` in the datasource block. Removed them from
`prisma/schema.prisma` and created `prisma.config.ts` (via
`defineConfig`) supplying `DIRECT_URL ?? DATABASE_URL` for
CLI/migrations.

All 530 unit tests pass after the repair.

## Files changed
- `lib/prisma.ts`
- `package-lock.json`
- `package.json`
- `prisma.config.ts`
- `prisma/schema.prisma`

## Diff summary
- 5 files changed, 1219 insertions(+), 63 deletions(-)

## Validation reported by the repair lane
- `npx prisma generate`
- `npm test`

## CI note
- Required checks are dispatched explicitly after PR creation so this
superseding PR becomes mergeable under the normal branch protection
rules.
- Review and merge remain human-owned.

commit.message:
Add graphics/image stub implementations for platforms without GFX

macOS libc mode doesn't include l_gfx.h so OSC_HAS_GFX is not defined.
Graphics functions were declared in osc_runtime.h but had no definitions,
causing linker errors on macOS CI.

Added #ifndef OSC_HAS_GFX stubs that compile but return errors at runtime
(canvas_open returns 'not supported'). Same for img_load without OSC_HAS_IMG.

72 tests pass, 28 examples compile.
